sections.paragraph:
i.q. صَبَغَ to dip in, to immerse, hence to dye, to tinge (cogn. to טָבַצ, which see); whence צֶבַע something dyed, and אֶצְבַּע a finger, especially the forefinger, so called from being dipped into things, although the Arab. أَصُبَعُ is written with ع.

II.

צבע

i.q. سبع to ravine as a wild beast; whence צָבוּעַ a ravenous beast, hyæna, Arab. سَبْعُ and سَبُعُ a lion; سِبَاغُ ravenous animals.
